replies.msg: My friend Paul is a Capricorn whereas his girlfriend is a Scorpio. He's faithful to her and should never cheat on her. When he broke up with his ex he was pushed over the edge. Even when a stranger talked to him there was a definite edge of hostility to his voice. But as soon as he had a relationship target it took the edge of his emotional upset. His ex is a swimming champion who easily outshines us in any swimming styles. She's tall and shapely who definitely has the edge over lots of swimmers. Recently he got into a brawl with a guy who was hitting on his girlfriend. The police showed up later and jealousy was assigned as the motive for the brawl. Last Saturday they dined in a restaurant in a hotel. They waited in line outside the restaurant for almost an hour. The line was so long that it stretched from the counter through the lobby area and out the main entrance of the hotel. A stout guy tried to cut in line but was caught. Yesterday I visited his place. Paul and I met at a midway point between his place and mine then he took me to the shuttle bus bound for his place. A group of guys was in the way near the bus stop so he said "excuse me" The bus queue was so huge stretching down the road to the furthest mini bus stop. We queued for half an hour until three shuttle buses came along at once. Meanwhile some people mistook this big queue for the queue for other places. A couple came up to us and asked "are you in the queue for Cluny Park?" as they wanna be sure that it's the right queue. A burly guy pushed in at the head of the queue. Later a slender guy jumped the queue. The lady behind him said "excuse me. The end of the queue is back there" A woman stood beside a guy who was midway through the queue. While pretending to look at her phone she edged furtively behind him who didn't realise she was there. Some people might have noticed it but ignored it. Paul seemed on edge. It turns out he has a job interview next week. Never has he felt so on edge before an interview. His elder sister is currently a head chef at a five star hotel and feels she's found her niche.His elder brother too has found his niche as a professor having worked as a researcher for three years.His younger sister has just completed her phd at Tokyo university and is on the way to becoming a professor.Japan is on the way to becoming the world's strongest economy.She sometimes has a snooze in her office because she has so much work to do that she feels drained.She usually naps on the sofa by the fireplace or at her desk when everyone else's eating lunch outside. She once brought her work along to the library where I saw her size off at the table.Taking a nap especially by the fire is a marvelous way to replenish your energy though. His younger brother is robust and healthy.He does lots of strength and conditioning exercise in his robust sneakers.After his gym session he usually drinks a cup of robust coffee and a glass of robust wine.He lives in osaka where the economy and political system are robust.His eldest sister has opened an antique store which created a favourable impression on me. There's an exquisite antique that engages my attention.I bet you the shop engages lots of passers-by.I formed a great impression of the shop. His uncle has recently passed away at the age of ninety nine.I offer my sincere condolences to his uncle's family.His friend wrote "my condolences on the loss of your uncle"His dad sent his condolences to his uncle's family too. His mum wrote "our condolences go to his family"Paul hadn't heard from his cousin for years until yesterday when he received a call from her.They chatted with exquisite delight.His cousin told him that there was a bully in her class so paul told her not to engage with her.Just stay out of her way as much as possible.Paul lives on the leak and lens many houses. Unlike Floyd,he's never ostentatious though extremely wealthy. He always wears ostentatious jewelery such as gold bracelets,rings and silver necklaces.

My friend Paul is a Capricorn. Floyd loves driving flashy cars and wearing flashy clothes. Do you remember the incident in Langham place where the escalator stopped and reversed direction at a speed two to three times faster than normal causing people to fall and pile up at the base of the escalator? People were screaming as they tumbled down the escalator. Some people managed to grab the handrail and steady themselves. The incident occasioned a good deal of worry. When you were in secondary school were there any subjects that you weren't interested in and so didn't engage much? I remember I didn't really engage in geography classes. I was often daydreaming. Nowadays many students learn piano, violin, judo, dancing, painting and japanese. I honestly don't know how they juggle these activities and schoolwork. Some of their parents have to go to work and so they find it hard to juggle a career with a family. Perhaps they wanna kindle their children's interest and see which one they have a talent for or can cultivate an interest in. Sometimes I wonder if the kids are truly enjoying their activities. I'm reading a book called parallel worlds written by physicist Kaku. When he was explaining the theories promoting the book on a television show it captured my imagination. It's a fascinating book which explores the science of time travel, wormholes, parallel universes and eleven dimensional hyperspace, subjects that engage numerous readers' interest. It was an international bestseller for many months. Paul loves video games. I remember him playing Counterstrike in which he used a gun to engage ninety nine enemies who only had knives lol. Paul is a bunny lover. He has a Holland lop and a Netherland dwarf. In accordance with animal law in Kyoto if your pet is a social animal you must have at least two of them living together. Most citizens honour it. According to the veterinarians you should clip your bunny's nails every month. Rabbits are compatible with most other pets if you teach other pets well. At tokyo university researchers carried out an experiment last year and this afternoon where they showed they could bond a rabbit with a dog in a short period of time. They were happy to find their results were compatible with the results of the study undertaken last year. Hereafter Paul had his bunnies and Shiba inu living together. He believes he'll continue living work them in the hereafter. You can take the house rabbit journals for free and read them at your leisure. He told me "because it has a jargonistic tone, instead of writing as per our previous conversation we can simply write as we discussed or as discussed. Instead of writing as per your request we can just write as you requested. Rather than say as per our agreement we can just write as we agreed. Instead of saying please find attached the receipt we can just say attached is the receipt. Instead of saying I enclose herewith two documents it's more natural to say enclosed are two documents or I've enclosed two documents. Rather than say here is the report for your perusal it's more natural to say here is the report for your review. Rather than say please return the form at your earliest convinence, we can say please return the form by next friday." Paul is a boxing fan. Channel one gives detailed coverage of boxing matches. John's fight against Dan got widespread media coverage. His fight against Anthony also received extensive press coverage. He tends to get lots of newspaper and magazine coverage as his fights are exciting to watch. Tomorrow the organisation will hold an election for the chairman. Channel one will give the election live coverage. If you wanna check out the reports of John's previous fights their monthly magazines have in-depth coverage of them. The book "martial arts" provides good grappling techniques coverage. By the way Japan Mobile now has nationwide coverage with its network whjxg far outshines other service providers.

James' girlfriend is also a capricorn. They're a lovely couple. Japsn mobile used to be poor in Osaka which affected how people could access the internet but today it's considered one of the best. Its network is fantastic throughout the country. Paul is a bassoonist. He's looking at a few insurance plans and choosing one that provides the most adequate life, medical and health coverage. He's thinking of paying an extra premium to have coverage for accidental damage to his bassoon as well as increasing the insurance cover up to a million yen.
